name: Impulsiveness
image_description: A wild figure on horseback gallops as if in a race. The symbol to the left is one of the greater seals symbolizing the six steps of creation, and is a protector of all things. The horseshoe symbolizes good fortune, resembling the bowl-like crescent of the waxing Moon.
personal: Mars is the ruling planet for Aries, and so here it sits in its natural fiery element. This planet/sign combination describes people who are energetic and impulsive in their every action. They feel a need to get things done, and this leads them to start numerous new projects. Acting before thinking inevitably leads to disasters, but these Martians are resilient and wins always predominate over losses. People described by this card are somewhat competitive and display a need to be seen in a leading position. Basically, they are outgoing and feel more comfortable at the front than with group endeavors. These Martians are natural leaders and take on this role within any situation which requires positive action. Mars/Aries people take great pride in their abilities, and are not happy unless they do more, and quicker, than anyone else. There is, however, a belligerence in the Mars/Aries nature that can go over the top, and these characters may ride roughshod over other people in order to get their own way. Mars/Aries personalities are at their best when they realize through experience that their potential for power works a great deal more effectively when they exert discreet control.
reading: When this card is drawn in a reading, it shows that some kind of physical and/or competitive action is necessary for the well-being of the person asking the question.
events: A challenge that requires courage, proving oneself by tackling the unknown, acting on impulse in a sudden situation; leading others with enthusiasm into a new venture.

name: Defense
image_description: Three mounted warriors, horses reined-in, take a defensive stance against attack. The Taurean image on the coin links positive action with material possessions. Six swords arranged in a pattern suggest they are there to impress, not to use.
personal: Much of the energy in the Mars/Taurus nature is devoted to the acquisition of material things. This card describes practical, determined people who will fight for and defend that which they deem important in life. People with this planet/sign combination take their time in making choices, but once the decision is made, there is no going back. Nobody is going to take away what belongs to the Mars Taurean – and if he or she gives things away, credit is required. Despite having a materialistic outlook, Mars Taureans can be incurable romantics, and rarely forget to keep the pleasure-loving part of life alive. They do not like to waste energy, and believe that skillful methods and techniques are worth more than brute force. Mars/Taurus types have a great ability to see the potential in the most ordinary things, transforming them with slow but sustained encouragement into objects of worth. For Mars Taureans, the option of running their own business is attractive. If they do, the chances of success are high, since a competitive streak makes them give of their best when completely responsible for their own finances. Negatively, this placing of Mars can indicate a ruthless and mercenary approach to life; in which all actions are guided by financial outcome.
reading: When this card is dealt in a reading, it shows a need to defend something the questioner values.
events: Starting up a new business, playing the stock market, a quick business deal with a profit; a possession that makes a leap in value; beating a competitor.

name: Decision
image_description: The chessboard symbolizes the mind and the attacking position the nature of the competitive planet Mars. The winged wheel denotes the Sun god, and movement in the element air or high mental activity. The doorway challenges a move into unknown territory.
personal: Most Mars/Gemini actions involve mental work and often manual dexterity too. Mars and Gemini alike are fast-moving factors in traditional astrology; neither planet nor sign can endure waiting around for things to happen, and both require quick action. This planet/sign combination indicates someone who is good in argument and debate, and brilliant at the quick verbal comeback. When it comes to sharp and aggressive repartee, Mars/Gemini types can lash with the tongue a little better than most. In fact, most of their battles are fought verbally. Such people display much inventiveness and ingenuity in their activities, which usually involve doing more than one job at a time. Often this placing of Mars indicates great versatility in work with the hands as well as with the mind. Coupled with Gemini's ability to do many things at the same time, it would be surprising if people with this aspect did not have a good, physical manipulative talent to add to their mental skills. Mars Geminians have a lot of energy to expend in rushing around gathering information, references or just good old-fashioned gossip, and are a source of information for all who enquire: if they don't know the answer personally, they know someone who does. If this person is not a professional reporter, he or she makes a good amateur.
reading: When this card is drawn, it indicates the need to make a quick decision, but bear in mind that speed is not the most important factor.
events: A heated argument, cross-questioning someone; a fight over a contract; a regretted letter.

name: Quarrel
image_description: Two mounted figures squabble over a gold ring symbolizing betrothal. This image concerns families united or divided over relationships or inheritance. The classical busts glare at each other, reminders that this has happened before.
personal: Mars Cancerians most enjoy expending energy and being active around the home or in a family-type business. However, the bossy nature of Mars cannot resist the temptation to take over and run things. In a family situation, this often leads to interminable and highly emotional disputes and arguments. The gentle sign of Cancer is not the most comfortable place to find pushy, aggressive Mars, and this card can indicate domestic upheaval. Mars acts immediately, and in the close atmosphere of home life, this causes much combative argument. Family matters will be stormy if Mars Cancerians try to get everyone to do things their way. The tricky situation to negotiate is that the Mars/Cancer person wants to be boss but also wants to be loved. It is best if people with this planet/sign type avoid bottling up emotions, as worries cause stomach upsets. Property can be important to the people this card describes, perhaps meaning more to them than just a place of their own. Home improvements are a constant demand on Mars Cancerians, and running some kind of business of their own, however modest, can offer a better outlet for Martian energy than quarreling. Mars Cancerians have a natural talent for property development, and can be lucky with real-estate deals given the slightest opportunity.
reading: When this card is selected, it usually shows that all is not well in the family nest.
events: A continuing family row, a family estrangement, fighting over an inheritance; a divorce or a problem involving custody.

name: Egotism
image_description: A strange bird – half-cockerel, half-man – crowned, stands proudly for all to see. Above is the Leo Sun and a royal Egyptian scarab to show the eminent position of the cockerel.
personal: The fiery planet Mars in the fiery sign Leo indicates that people with this planet/sign aspect will be most fulfilled using their energy in expansive and showy creative ways. Doing things on a big scale has appeal for this character, more especially if an audience is involved. The tendency to take over and lead others is also strong for this personality type. This dynamic combination of planet and sign inevitably indicates a powerful mutual attraction with the opposite sex. Mars Leos have a strong sex drive that needs plenty of activity and appreciation. A great deal of this drive and passion could manifest itself as creative talent, and would be worthwhile developing. Competitive sports and games have great appeal to people with this planet/sign combination, and they usually have a talent for working with children in an entertaining or creative way. With Leo's entrepreneurial abilities, this card often indicates a talent for coaching the young in sports – at which Mars Leos can excel. The need for appreciation and admiration is strong, however, and the Mars/Leo personality makes for a great deal of showing off and over-the-top behavior. Negatively, this may appear to others as authoritative and dictatorial.
reading: When this card is drawn in a reading, questioners are advised to look at themselves and their current behavior with regard to the question. The Egotism card may indicate that they need to be less bullying and to do more to earn the respect of others.
events: A dramatic performance, a great creative enterprise; forcing oneself into the lead, bullying others.

name: Criticism
image_description: A blue eye stares fixedly at an arrangement of flowers in a simple basket. Above, a finger points accusingly at the necklace of a woman who has a hair-braid made of wheat.
personal: Mars/Virgo energy can find a good outlet in skilled work and craftsmanship. Many people with this planet/sign combination are attracted to medicine, and especially areas such as surgery in which great care and precision is necessary. They also often are skilled with machinery and motors. If the occupation of the Mars Virgoan does not allow this ability for precision or detail work to be expressed, it can manifest and provide great satisfaction as a specialist spare-time interest. Virgo always like to plan things carefully, not liking to fritter away energies, and although Mars usually provides an excess of energy, in this sign it is well-directed and never wasted. This card indicates a propensity for success and even prominence in work for a well-run organization, probably in areas in which analysis and study of health, medicine, complementary healing or dietary control is important. Mars Virgoans tend to be perfectionists, and may set themselves impossible standards. In extreme cases, there is a danger that they can make themselves uncomfortable or even ill with this self-inflicted pressure. Often, it manifests in superficial behavior towards others, picking on faults to compensate for what the Mars Virgoan feels to be his or her own failure.
reading: In a reading, this card indicates a need to be more critical in providing the answer to the question, or, conversely, that one is being overly critical to the detriment of the situation.
events: A co-operative research project; a position of responsibility in a large organization, a personal assistant to an important person.

name: Choice
image_description: There are two women. One, blindfolded, is Justice and holds the scales; the other watches the swing of the balance. The rose weighs the beauty of the flower against the sharpness of the thorns. The leg symbol suggests difficulty in movement.
personal: Libra is the opposite sign to Aries, the natural sign of Mars (see page 84), and so this planet/sign combination always brings with it strong mood swings. People with this aspect focus a great deal of energy into co-operative and partnership work. They appreciate the company of energetic people and throw themselves into group projects with enthusiasm. They play by the rules and expect the same from others. The competitive nature of Mars combined with the diplomatic qualities of Libra means that the people this card describes probably need a good argument to release pressure. Though they like partnerships, Mars Librans do, however unconsciously, see themselves as the boss on a competitive level. They tend, in fact, to pick themselves sparring partners. As long as their chosen ones know the rules of the game, all is well, but if they do not, the ride may be a stormy one. As with all matters of the Balance, once the scales are tipped, there will undoubtedly be a great deal of swinging back and forth in every activity these personality types embark on. Many Mars/Libra actions are taken on impulse where they concern others, and people with this planet/sign aspect must be prepared to make a few grand mistakes. Tact and diplomacy may come a little harder with the red planet in this sign, but are more likely to succeed in the end.
reading: When this card appears, it always indicates a difficult choice to be made.
events: A deliberate change of direction; a business merger, choosing between two partners or diverse business options.

name: Revenge
image_description: A heroic figure holds an enemy's head. His nakedness shows vulnerability, the helmet and sword his warrior rank. A winged figure brings a fiery torch in honor of the action. Above, two lovers, Sun and Moon, embrace.
personal: Mars is the old ruler of Scorpio, and those with this planet/sign combination need to be faced with a real challenge to warrant putting their great energy into action. Once they believe a thing to be worthwhile, however, the amount of sheer energy they make available to achieve their aim is phenomenal. But there is a danger that the current passion of a Mars/Scorpio personality can be used to bulldoze and overpower other people. The temper of people with this planet/sign aspect will always be a little over the top; they never remain in the grey area in the middle when one of two extreme positions can be chosen. All or nothing are Mars/Scorpio personalities! They strongly invest their desires and emotional intensity in every action, and make available a great deal of aggressive drive for joint ventures. Mars Scorpions have the power to transform or change almost impossible situations, and in fact may well do this several times during the course of their life. Devotedly loyal to those they choose to spend their life with, Mars/Scorpio types are equally passionate in hating those who slight them in any way, in a case of don't get mad, get even!
reading: When this card is selected in a reading, extreme action is at least a possibility in the current situation. As long as one is able to use this potential in a controlled way, the outcome can be positive.
events: A phenomenal achievement, extreme effort in order to overcome some kind of problem; getting even with someone, an over-the-top reaction to a perceived hurt.

name: Domination
image_description: Two naked young men wrestle in a field outside a solid-looking house. One has forced the other into a yielding position to the point of defeat. Above are the three symbols of the great religions descended from the prophet Abraham.
personal: Mars Sagittarians devote a great deal of energy to crusading and the conversion of others to their beliefs. Staunch defenders of the truth, they can be a little blunt in putting their case to others. Because of the excessive power in this planet/sign combination, some sporting or adventurous activity benefits people with this aspect at every stage of life. Foreign travel could be a satisfactory outlet for pioneering energies, and it would be surprising if Mars Sagittarians did not spend some time abroad, or with people from another country. Active participation in some religious or philosophical cause is indicated by this card. Negatively, this can manifest as a narrow and limited view of life into which the Mars Sagittarian tries to shoehorn everyone – not a winning strategy in the popularity stakes. As long as Mars Sagittarians do not get too dogmatic and can maintain an "it-takes-all-sorts" attitude, all is well. With Mars in Sagittarius, the competitive instinct is irrepressible, and the need to come out on top in any situation can become excessive.
reading: When this card is chosen in a reading it can mean, depending on the question asked, that either the questioner is being pressured by someone or is applying too much pressure to someone else. The Domination card indicates a need to stand back and detach oneself in order to see the truth of a situation.
events: An argument over ideals; persuading others by bullying; the start of an expedition; reckless optimism with regard to a long-term project; competitive sports.

name: Authority
image_description: A king stands firm, holding symbols of authority and power. The orb with the globe topped by a cross represents traditionally the church's power over the world. Here, the cross is the equal-sided cross of earth: the power of the material world challenges the spiritual.
personal: Those people with Mars in Capricorn have great energy when it is channeled into professional ambitions, and have a strong desire for recognition and status. The actions of Mars/Capricorn people are always well considered and decisive. Not given to rushing into things willy-nilly, their energies are rarely wasted on impulsive and costly mistakes. They show initiative and the executive ability that makes practical achievements possible. This placing of Mars gives undoubted potential for constructive and energetic leadership in whichever field Mars/Capricorn people make their own. The negative side of this card can manifest as a "power-at-all-costs" attitude where career and ambition are concerned. In such situations, Mars Capricornians have little care for those pushed aside in their own drive for achievement. However, this attitude rarely results in the kind of recognition that Mars/Capricorn types find important for fulfillment. Fortunately, the fiery planet Mars is usually tempered in the sign of Capricorn, and power is applied with a great deal of common sense and control, which manifests as responsible action.
reading: When this card appears, it can indicate a need for the questioner to take control of the situation by asserting authority. Alternatively, it may be necessary to seek the help of a person in power.
events: Ambitious moves requiring great self-control; being in a position of power over less reliable people; a lonely job with great responsibility; seeking help from someone in a position of authority.

name: Rebellion
image_description: A walled fortress town sits solid and secure beneath dark and brooding clouds. Above, a young warrior challenges with his sword two giant bearded nobles symbolizing tradition and established authority.
personal: Freedom of action is of utmost importance to Mars/Aquarius. Rarely following old methods blindly, people with this planet/sign combination do things their way, which is probably in a quite innovative and unorthodox style. Mars Aquarians happily expend their energies on the new, novel and scientific, and they have a natural talent with gadgets which could develop into genuine inventive talent. However it goes, Mars Aquarians will invent something. The company of friends is of enormous importance to people with this aspect, and they are more likely to be strong, energetic and male – willing supporters too. People described by this card are probably not quiet when it comes to showing their discontent with outmoded or unfair social attitudes, and it is hard to find Mars Aquarians who do not have a good humanitarian cause to battle for. The Mars Aquarian's odd-ball outlook on life makes it easy for them to examine anything established or traditional to see whether its rules and regulations are still viable. They are rarely afraid to express views that are not the norm, and show great courage in following their beliefs against all odds in order to overturn a situation which they believe is unfair or illogical.
reading: Receiving this card in a reading indicates a need for a lateral approach to the question. Turn the current thinking on its head and approach it from a different angle – a drastic change of thought is needed.
events: Leading a protest, expressing discontent with authority, the courage to be different; anger over indifference.

name: Escape
image_description: A woman in a chariot pulled by tigers escapes from something she has to turn back and see. Servants accompany her flight. On the seal above, a boy rides a dolphin, symbol of the power of the sea. The Buddha suggests escape from the material world.
personal: Those people with the Mars/Pisces combination are dedicated to understanding, sympathy and sacrifice. They use their idealistic energies in visionary and sensitive ways. A great deal of their powers are thrown into the development of the Piscean imagination, which, if they are fortunate to be able to share it with others, becomes a very fulfilling part of life. If Mars Pisceans have the necessary ability to develop their imaginations as an artistic talent, they will have unlimited scope for creativity. People with this planet/sign combination always feel a great need to help others in caring ways. This may involve working in positions and situations in which they take on a supportive, background role, and often this card indicates work for or within a large institution, usually in one of the caring professions. The Mars in Pisces combination highlights a personality that can be somewhat secretive, and could involve a secret love, a plot or even hidden enemies.
reading: When this card is drawn in a reading, it may show that the questioner's desire to please others to the exclusion of personal needs has placed him or her within a complex and uncomfortable relationship or situation. Mars in Pisces is very rarely a fighting aspect and more often than not, eventually, when all other options fail, leads one to escape from a situation by any means available.
events: Using great energy to extract oneself from a tricky situation; working extremely hard for the benefit of others; shutting one's eyes to the reality of a situation.
